Origin Wukong, named after the Monkey King from Chinese mythology, represents China's leading entry into the third generation of superconducting quantum computers. It was made accessible to global users on January 6, and since then, it has attracted remote visitors from 61 countries, with American users leading the tally, as reported by the Science and Technology Daily, a publication of China's Ministry of Science and Technology.

Origin Quantum, the company behind Origin Wukong, was founded in 2017 by quantum physicists Guo Guoping and Guo Guangcan from the University of Science and Technology of China (USTC) in Anhui province.

occurrence_sentence: Visitors from the United States are at the top of the list of users of the technology, according to Chinese state media, as reported in the South China Morning Post.
China's independently developed, cutting-edge quantum computer, Origin Wukong, has seen a surge in usage since its opening to international users just 10 days ago.

Origin Quantum, the company behind Origin Wukong, was founded in 2017 by quantum physicists Guo Guoping and Guo Guangcan from the University of Science and Technology of China (USTC) in Anhui province.
